data.content:
Generated Code
Generated code is the output produced by AI tools, which may not always be correct and might have room for improvement in terms of structure and efficiency.

AI Tools
AI tools are software applications that can assist in generating code and potentially help avoid syntax errors.

Program Logic
Program logic refers to the underlying reasoning and flow of a computer program, where errors must be identified through testing.

Syntax Errors
Syntax errors are mistakes in the code's grammar or structure that AI tools may help avoid.

Comprehensive Documented Testing
Comprehensive documented testing is a method used to identify errors in program logic and ensure the correctness of code.

Validation Standards
Validation standards are criteria that submissions must meet to be considered acceptable.

Separation of Concerns
Separation of concerns is a principle in software design that suggests code should be organized so that each part addresses a distinct responsibility, often a scope for improvement in generated code.

Attribution Standards
Attribution standards are guidelines that submissions must meet, ensuring proper credit and acknowledgment.

Avoiding Repetitions
Avoiding repetitions is a coding principle aimed at reducing redundancy in code, often identified as an area for improvement in generated code.

Final Exam
A comprehensive exam worth 30% of the final grade.<SEP>A comprehensive exam contributing 30% to the overall course grade.

Quizzes
Quizzes contribute 5% each to the overall course grade, totaling 20%.

Final Exam Schedule
The schedule for the comprehensive final exam: May 4, from 3:30 - 5:30 PM in HRBB 113.<SEP>The final exam is scheduled from 3:30 PM to 5:30 PM on May 4.

Homework
Individual assignments subject to collaboration and late day policies.

Submission
The submission is the final work presented, which must adhere to specific standards of attribution and validation.

Final Exam Location
The final exam will be held in HRBB 113.

Homework Collaboration Policy
The policy stating that all homework assignments are individual assignments.<SEP>Specifies that Homeworks are individual assignments, encourages discussion of approaches and methods, but prohibits pair programming, code sharing, or working through specific problems in code together. All help must be acknowledged.

Regrade Policy
Allows students seven days from receiving a graded assignment to request a regrade in writing to the instructor. The policy reserves the right to regrade the entire assignment.

Instructor
The recipient of written regrade requests according to the Regrade Policy.

Late Work Policy
Defines rules for late submissions, exempting makeup work for excused absences as per Student Rule 7.

Student Rule 7
A student rule referenced by the Late Work Policy concerning excused absences.

Course Specific Late Work Policy
Provides specific details for the late day policy applied to Homework assignments.

Homework Late Day Policy
Allows each student five total late days for Homeworks, defined as indivisible 24-hour units. After exhaustion of late days, late assignments will receive a grade of 0.

Course Schedule
The Course Schedule provides a rough outline of weekly topics and important dates, which is subject to change.<SEP>A rough schedule outlining course topics by week, subject to change.

Course Overview
Course Overview is the topic covered in Week 1 of the course.<SEP>A topic covered in Week 1 of the course schedule.

Group Project
A mini-research project where students work in teams of 3 or 4, contributing to the Group Project Grade Component.<SEP>A mini-research project where students work in teams of 3 or 4, contributing 20% to the overall course grade.

Boolean Retrieval
Boolean Retrieval is a topic covered in Week 1 of the course.<SEP>A topic covered in Week 1 of the course schedule.

TF-IDF
TF-IDF is a topic covered in Week 2 of the course.<SEP>A topic covered in Week 2 of the course schedule.

Vector Space Model
Vector Space Model is a topic covered in Week 2 of the course.<SEP>A topic covered in Week 2 of the course schedule.

Link Analysis
A topic covered in Week 3 of the course schedule.

BM25
BM25 is a topic covered in Week 2 of the course.<SEP>A topic covered in Week 2 of the course schedule.

Evaluation Of Search Engines
A topic covered in Week 4 of the course schedule.

Learning To Rank
An algorithm related to web search systems to be learned.<SEP>A topic covered in Week 5 of the course schedule.

Recommender Systems
Key concepts and models related to recommender systems are a central topic.<SEP>A topic covered in Week 6 of the course schedule, encompassing Content-Based and Collaborative Filtering.

Content-Based Recommender Systems
A sub-topic of Recommender Systems covered in Week 6.

Collaborative Filtering
A key concept and model relevant to recommender systems to be defined and explained.<SEP>A sub-topic of Recommender Systems covered in Week 6.

Course Grading Scale
The policy defining the percentage ranges for letter grades (A, B, C, D, F) in the course.

In-Class Quizzes
Four brief quizzes (each 5%) contributing to the Quizzes Grade Component.

Programming Assignments
Five individual programming assignments (weighted 2%, 7%, 7%, 7%, 7%) completed in Python using Jupyter Notebook, contributing to the Homework Grade Component.

Adversarial Information Retrieval
A recent research result in web search and recommender systems to be identified and applied.

Implicit Recommendation
A key concept and model relevant to recommender systems to be defined and explained.

Jupyter Notebook
A tool used for the first programming assignment to familiarize students with its operations.

Matrix Factorization
A key concept and model relevant to recommender systems to be defined and explained.

data.entity_name: Neural Models Of Retrieval And Recommendation
data.content:
Neural Models Of Retrieval And Recommendation
A recent research result in web search and recommender systems to be identified and applied.

PageRank Algorithm
A specific algorithm related to web search systems to be learned.

Python Programming Language
The programming language required for all programming assignments.

Homework Grade Component
A component of the final grade, weighted at 30%, consisting of five programming assignments.

Recommender System Evaluation
A key concept and model relevant to recommender systems to be defined and explained.

Quizzes Grade Component
A component of the final grade, weighted at 20%, consisting of four brief in-class quizzes.

Group Project Grade Component
A component of the final grade, weighted at 20%, involving a mini-research project.

Final Exam Grade Component
A component of the final grade, weighted at 30%, consisting of a comprehensive final exam.

HRBB 113
The specific room location for the Final Exam.<SEP>HRBB 113 is the designated meeting location for CSCE 670 lectures.

data.entity_name: Apply Recent Research In Recommender Systems
data.content:
Apply Recent Research In Recommender Systems
Ability to identify salient features and apply recent research results in recommender systems, including adversarial information retrieval and neural models of recommendation.

data.entity_name: Apply Recent Research In Web Search
data.content:
Apply Recent Research In Web Search
Ability to identify salient features and apply recent research results in web search, including adversarial information retrieval and neural models of retrieval.

data.entity_name: Design And Evaluate Recommendation System Algorithms
data.content:
Design And Evaluate Recommendation System Algorithms
Ability to design, implement, and evaluate the core algorithms underlying a fully functional recommendation system.

data.entity_name: Design And Evaluate Web Search System Algorithms
data.content:
Design And Evaluate Web Search System Algorithms
Ability to design, implement, and evaluate the core algorithms underlying a fully functional web search system.

Christopher D Manning
An author of the optional textbook "Introduction To Information Retrieval".

Hinrich Schutze
An author of the optional textbook "Introduction To Information Retrieval".

Jeffrey D Ullman
An author of the optional textbook "Mining Of Massive Datasets".

Jure Leskovec
An author of the optional textbook "Mining Of Massive Datasets".

Anand Rajaraman
An author of the optional textbook "Mining Of Massive Datasets".

Prabhakar Raghavan
An author of the optional textbook "Introduction To Information Retrieval".

Introduction To Information Retrieval
An optional textbook for the course, authored by Christopher D. Manning, Prabhakar Raghavan, and Hinrich Schutze, published in 2009, and available online.

Mining Of Massive Datasets
An optional textbook for the course, authored by Jure Leskovec, Anand Rajaraman, and Jeffrey D. Ullman, published in 2014, and available online.

Web Search System
A system for which core algorithms are designed, implemented, and evaluated, and to which recent research results are applied.

The Course
The course covers various topics including information retrieval, search engines, recommender systems, and large language models.

Late Assignment Policy
The Late Assignment Policy stipulates that any late assignment will receive a grade of 0.

AI Policy
The AI Policy allows submission of AI-generated code if properly documented with the prompt and significant parts of the response, while emphasizing student responsibility for error identification and improvement.

Evaluation of Search Engines
A topic covered in Week 4 of the course schedule.

Learning to Rank
A topic covered in Week 5 of the course schedule.

Recommender Systems (Content-Based)
A topic on recommender systems, focusing on content-based methods, covered in Week 6 of the course schedule.

Recommender Systems (Collaborative Filtering)
A topic on recommender systems, focusing on collaborative filtering, covered in Week 6 of the course schedule.

Recommender Systems (Bayesian Personalized Ranking)
A topic on recommender systems, focusing on Bayesian personalized ranking, covered in Week 7 of the course schedule.

Recommender Systems (Matrix Factorization)
A topic on recommender systems, focusing on matrix factorization, covered in Week 7 of the course schedule.

CSCE 670
CSCE 670, titled "Information Storage and Retrieval," is a 3-credit hour course offered in Spring 2026.

Word2vec
A topic on word embeddings, covered in Week 8 of the course schedule.

Yu Zhang
Yu Zhang is the instructor for CSCE 670, with an email of yuzhang@tamu.edu, office at PETR 222, and phone number (217) 954-3457.

CSCE 670 Meeting Schedule
Lectures for CSCE 670 are held on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays from 1:50 PM to 2:40 PM, starting January 12, 2026, and ending May 05, 2026, at HRBB 113.

Yu Zhang Office Hours
Instructor Yu Zhang holds office hours on Mondays from 3:30 PM to 4:30 PM.

CSCE 670 Catalog Description
The CSCE 670 catalog description covers representation, storage, access to multimedia document collections, fundamental data structures and algorithms, and techniques to design and evaluate retrieval systems including indexing, compressing, and querying.

CSCE 310
CSCE 310 is a course that serves as a prerequisite for CSCE 670.

CSCE 603
CSCE 603 is a course that serves as a prerequisite for CSCE 670.

CSCE 670 Approval of Instructor
Approval of the instructor is a prerequisite condition for enrolling in CSCE 670.

CSCE 670 Graduate Classification
Graduate classification is a prerequisite status required for enrolling in CSCE 670.

data.entity_name: CSCE 670 Learning Outcome: Web Search Concepts
data.content:
CSCE 670 Learning Outcome: Web Search Concepts
Upon completion of CSCE 670, learners will be able to define and explain key concepts and models relevant to web search, such as text indexing, retrieval models, evaluation, Web crawling, PageRank, and learning to rank.

data.entity_name: CSCE 670 Learning Outcome: System Design and Evaluation
data.content:
CSCE 670 Learning Outcome: System Design and Evaluation
Upon completion of CSCE 670, learners will be able to design, implement, and evaluate the core algorithms underlying functional web search and recommendation systems.

data.entity_name: CSCE 670 Learning Outcome: Recommender Systems Concepts
data.content:
CSCE 670 Learning Outcome: Recommender Systems Concepts
Upon completion of CSCE 670, learners will be able to define and explain key concepts and models relevant to recommender systems, including collaborative filtering, matrix factorization, evaluation, and implicit recommendation.

CSCE 670 Learning Outcome: Research Application
Upon completion of CSCE 670, learners will be able to identify salient features and apply recent research results in web search and recommender systems, including adversarial information retrieval.

CSCE 670 Section 600
Section 600 (CRN 46627) is a specific offering of the CSCE 670 course.

College Station
College Station is the campus location where the CSCE 670 course is held.

Spring 2026
Spring 2026 is the academic term during which CSCE 670 is offered.

CSCE 670 Syllabus
This document serves as the official course syllabus for CSCE 670.

Lecture
Lecture (LEC) is the type of meeting for CSCE 670.

PETR 222
PETR 222 is the office location for Instructor Yu Zhang.

data.entity_name: Define Recommender Systems Concepts And Models
data.content:
Define Recommender Systems Concepts And Models
Ability to define and explain the key concepts and models relevant to recommender systems.
